## Recommended Reading {.unnumbered}

* **Fisher, R. A. (1935). *The Design of Experiments.* Oliver & Boyd.**  
  A foundational text that introduced the principles of randomization and experimental control, forming the basis of modern statistical design.

* **Montgomery, D. C. (2017). *Design and Analysis of Experiments* (9th ed.). John wiley & sons.**  
  A comprehensive and accessible modern treatment of experimental design, emphasizing applications and interpretation.

*  **Endrenyl, L. (1978). *Statistics. by David Freedman, Robert Pisani, Roger Purves*. WW Norton & Co.**  
This book emphasizes clear reasoning, real examples, and conceptual insight rather than formula memorization. 

* **Ross, S. M. (2020). *A First Course in Probability*. Pearson.**
A widely used undergraduate text that builds a solid foundation through clear explanations and a broad range of exercises.  

* **Blitzstein, J. K., & Hwang, J. (2019). *Introduction to Probability*. Chapman and Hall/CRC**.
An engaging, example-driven introduction that emphasizes problem-solving and intuition. 


* **Feller, W. (1971). *An Introduction to Probability Theory and Its Applications, Volume 1*. Wiley.**
A rigorous classic that provides a deeper and more formal treatment of the subject.
